expectations.text: Verified currency of all 5 actions using live sources (GitHub API)
expectations.evidence: Verification log shows WebFetch calls to api.github.com for all 5 actions: 'checkout latest release | WebFetch | api.github.com/repos/actions/checkout/releases/latest | v6.0.2', 'upload-artifact latest release | WebFetch | api.github.com/repos/actions/upload-artifact/releases/latest | v7.0.0', 'download-artifact latest release | WebFetch | api.github.com/repos/actions/download-artifact/releases/latest | v8.0.1', 'cache latest release | WebFetch | api.github.com/repos/actions/cache/releases/latest | v5.0.4', 'setup-node latest release | WebFetch | api.github.com/repos/actions/setup-node/releases/latest | v6.3.0'. Self-check also states: 'Every version sourced from a live lookup: Yes — all versions fetched from GitHub API, not training data'.

expectations.text: Fetched commit SHAs for all 5 actions and presented them in pinned format (owner/action@sha # vX.Y.Z)
expectations.evidence: SHA Pinning Reference section provides all 5 actions in the required format: 'actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2', 'actions/upload-artifact@bbbca2ddaa5d8feaa63e36b76fdaad77386f024f # v7.0.0', 'actions/download-artifact@3e5f45b2cfb9172054b4087a40e8e0b5a5461e7c # v8.0.1', 'actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7 # v5.0.4', 'actions/setup-node@53b83947a5a98c8d113130e565377fae1a50d02f # v6.3.0'. SHAs were sourced via WebFetch from api.github.com git/refs/tags endpoints as documented in the verification log.

expectations.text: Covered security advisories for all 5 actions — bundling actions/* into shared searches is acceptable if explicitly documented in the log
expectations.evidence: Each of the 5 actions has its own dedicated security search row in the verification log: 'checkout security advisories | WebSearch | "actions/checkout CVE security advisory 2025 2026"', 'upload-artifact security advisories | WebSearch | "actions/upload-artifact CVE security advisory 2025 2026"', 'download-artifact security advisories | WebSearch | "actions/download-artifact security advisory CVE 2025 2026"', 'cache security advisories | WebSearch | "actions/cache CVE security advisory 2025 2026"', 'setup-node security advisories | WebSearch | "actions/setup-node CVE security advisory 2025 2026"'. All 5 dependencies have individual security search coverage — no bundling.

expectations.text: Verification log documents which security search covers which dependencies when bundled
expectations.evidence: No bundling was performed — each of the 5 actions received its own dedicated, individually-named security search row in the verification log. Because searches are 1:1 with dependencies, there is no bundling ambiguity to document. The self-check confirms: 'Security search entries match dependency count (5): Yes — checkout, upload-artifact, download-artifact, cache, setup-node each searched via WebSearch and GitHub Advisory Database'.

expectations.text: Included a verification log table at the end
expectations.evidence: The report includes a '## Verification Log' section near the end containing a markdown table with columns 'Claim | Tool | Source | Finding', covering all 5 actions with multiple rows each (releases, tags, SHA lookups, changelogs, security searches). The Self-Check section follows after the table.

eval_feedback.overall: The report fully satisfies all 5 assertions. All 5 actions were verified via live GitHub API calls with individual WebFetch entries per action. SHAs are presented in the required owner/action@sha # vX.Y.Z format. Security advisory searches are individually scoped 1:1 per dependency with no bundling. The verification log table is present and comprehensive. The self-check section further confirms completeness.
